properties.remark: OFF-DUTY NWS METEOROLOGIST REPORTED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA AND PHONE CALL THAT FLASH FLOODING WAS CURRENTLY ONGOING AT THE INTERSECTION OF SE 9TH AVENUE AND SE 1ST STREET IN DOWNTOWN MIAMI. VIDEO FOOTAGE SHOWS SEVERAL INCHES OF STANDING WATER ON THE ROADWAY/CURB AS WELL AS WATER ENTERING THE FIRST FLOOR OF BUILDINGS. WATER IS HALFWAY UP CAR TIRES IN THE VIDEO ALSO WELL.

SEVERAL PICTURES AND VIDEOS RECEIVED OF A WATERSPOUT WEST OF MARCO ISLAND AND ISLES OF CAPRI MOVING TOWARDS SHORE. RADAR DATA SUGGESTS THIS IS THE SAME STORM THAT MOVED ONSHORE WITH THE APPARENT TORNADO IN THE TAMIAMI TRAIL/SR 951 AREA.

SEVERAL PICTURES AND VIDEOS RECEIVED OF AN APPARENT TORNADO JUST WEST OF THE ARTESIA COMMUNITY SW OF THE INTERSECTION OF TAMIAMI TRAIL/US 41 AND COLLIER BLVD (SR 951). RADAR DATA INDICATED ROTATION IN THIS AREA AND THE TIME IS BASED ON THIS. ANOTHER PICTURE WAS RECEIVED OF AN APPARENT TORNADO NEAR THE US 41/SR 951 INTERSECTION WHICH IS PROBABLY THIS SAME TORNADO.
FOR ADDITIONAL DETAILS SEE PUBLIC INFORMATION STATEMENT (PNS).
CORRECTED AFTER RECEIVING NEW VIDEO FOOTAGE. SOCIAL MEDIA INDICATED SEMI-TRUCK FLIPPED ON I-75 NEAR MILE MARKER 96 DUE TO A TORNADO CROSSING I-75.
CORRECTED TO TORNADO AFTER RECEIVING NEW VIDEO FOOTAGE. SOCIAL MEDIA INDICATED SEMI-TRUCK FLIPPED ON I-75 NEAR MILE MARKER 96 DUE TO A TORNADO CROSSING I-75.
TWITTER VIDEO SHOWED A CONFIRMED TORNADO ON THE GROUND NEAR THE ARTESIA COMMUNITY IN NAPLES.
SOCIAL MEDIA INDICATED SEMI-TRUCK FLIPPED ON I-75 NEAR MILE MARKER 96 DUE TO HIGH WINDS IN THE AREA.
A MARINE THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 47MPH / 41 KNOTS WAS RECORDED BY THE WXFLOW MESONETESONET STATION XGVT GOVERNMENT CUT.

LEANING POWER POLE OBSERVED ALONG TAMIAMI TRAIL JUST EAST OF THE INTERSECTION WITH US-29. DAMAGE WAS TORNADIC IN NATURE AND MATCHES WITH RADAR VELOCITY/REFLECTIVITY AT THE TIME AS A STRONG CIRCULATION PASSED OVER.
A MARINE THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 44MPH / 38 KNOTS WAS RECORDED BY THE WXFLOW MESONET STATION XJUP JUNO BEACH PIER.
PUBLIC REPORTED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA A DOWNED TREE AT THE UNIVERSITY OF MIAMI NEAR DICKINSON DR. AS A RESULT OF A SEVERE STORM MOVING THROUGH THE AREA. RADAR ESTIMATED TIME IS ROUGHLY 12:47 PM.
A MARINE THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 47MPH / 41 KNOTS WAS RECORDED BY THE WXFLOW MESONET STATION XPEG PORT EVERGLADES.
A MARINE THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 44MPH / 38 KNOTS WAS RECORDED BY THE WXFLOW MESONET STATION XKBS BISCAYNE BAY LIGHT 20.
BROADCAST MEDIA REPORTED (VIA SOCIAL MEDIA) A DOWNED TREE AND POWERLINES ON BUCHANAN ST. IN HOLLYWOOD AS A RESULT OF A SEVERE STORM MOVING THROUGH THE AREA. RADAR ESTIMATED TIME IS ROUGHLY 12:58PM.
UMIAMI RSMAS STATION ON VIRGINIA KEY REPORTED A WIND GUST OF 68 KNOTS ASSOCIATED WITH A LINE OF STRONG THUNDERSTORMS.
PUBLIC SHARED VIDEO VIA SOCIAL MEDIA OF FLASH FLOODING IN DOWNTOWN FORT LAUDERDALE SHOWING WATER ABOVE THE CURBS, OVER THE SIDEWALKS, AND ENTERING GROUND FLOOR STORE FRONTS.
PUBLIC REPORTED FLASH FLOODING OF THEIR NEIGHBORHOOD STREETS IN DANIA BEACH VIA SOCIAL MEDIA. WATER WAS APPROACHING THE SIDEWALKS IN FRONT OF THE HOUSES.
AN OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED QUARTER SIZED HAIL FROM A THUNDERSTORM ALONG US 41 IN WESTERN MIAMI DADE COUNTY.
A WIND GUST OF 53 MPH (46 KT) ASSOCIATED WITH A STRONG THUNDERSTORM MOVING OFFSHORE WAS MEASURED BY THE MESONET STATION XKBS BISCAYNE BAY LIGHT 20. STATION ELEVATION: 20 FT.
MESONET STATION XJUP JUNO BEACH PIER.
MESONET STATION XJUP JUNO BEACH PIER REPORTED A GUST TO 34 KNOTS.

TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTS QUARTER SIZED HAIL ON 154TH TERRACE SOUTH OF TMB IN COUNTRY WALK.

EMERGENCY MANAGERS REPORTED HAIL UP TO THE SIZE OF QUARTERS. CAR REPORTED DENTED FROM LARGE HAIL ALONG HWY 80 AT THE BORDER OF GLADES AND HENDRY COUNTIES.

GUST TO 41 MPH AT GOVERNMENT CUT WEATHERFLOW SITE (XGVT).
WATERSPOUT. VIDEO RECEIVED FROM WPEC VIA SOCIAL MEDIA OF A WATERSPOUT ON LAKE MANGONIA IN WEST PALM BEACH. THE WATERSPOUT DID NOT DO ANY DAMAGE OR CROSS OVER INTO ADJANCENT LAND AREAS.

PICTURES AND VIDEO RECEIVED ON SOCIAL MEDIA VIA WPTV AND WPEC OF SIGNIFICANT FLOODING IN WEST PALM BEACH ALONG N. FLAGLER DRIVE IN THE 31ST STREET AREA. SEVERAL CARS WERE STALLED DUE TO THE DEEP WATER ON THE STREET. A RAINFALL MEASUREMENT OF 2.36 INCHES WAS RECEIVED IN THE VICINITY, WITH RAINFALL ESTIMATES OF 2.5 TO 3 INCHES IN JUST ONE HOUR.
EARTH NETWORKS MESONET PHOKE PAHOKEE HIGH SCHOOL RECORDED A WIND GUST OF 40MPH, OR 35KTS AT 6:18PM AS A STRONG THUNDERSTORM MOVED ONSHORE FROM LAKE OKEECHOBEE. SITE ELEVATION IS LISTED AS 13 FEET.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TSTM WND GST REPORT FROM 7 WNW INLIKITA. MESONET STATION CHKF1 CHEKIKA REPORTED A WIND GUST OF 58MPH AT 6:34 PM AS A SEVERE THUNDERSTORM MOVED THROUGH THE AREA. SITE ELEVATION IS LISTED AS 4 FEET.
MESONET STATION CHKF1 CHEKIKA REPORTED A WIND GUST OF 58MPH AT 7:23PM AS A SEVERE THUNDERSTORM MOVED THROUGH THE AREA. SITE ELEVATION IS LISTED AS 4 FEET.
AT 2:30 PM, A LOCAL RESTAURANT ON THE INTRACOASTAL IN LANTANA REPORTED DAMAGE FROM STRONG WINDS. A FLAG POLE WAS BROKEN IN HALF AND A TIKI ROOF WAS BLOWN OFF.

MEDIUM-SIZED TREE SNAPPED AT THE TRUNK IN THE AREA OF SPANISH RIVER BLVD AND NW 2 AVE IN BOCA RATON. VIDEO RECEIVED VIA WPTV-TV SHOWING STRONG WIND GUSTS OVER 50 MPH. RADAR SHOWED WINDS PROBABLY IN THE 60 MPH OR GREATER RANGE FROM A WELL DEFINED DOWNBURST SIGNATURE. TREE APPEARED TO SHOW SIGNS OF ROTTING IN THE TRUNK. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON WHEN REPORTS WERE RECEIVED AS WELL AS RADAR DATA.

REPORTS OF SIGNIFICANT FLOODING IN THE NW 2 AVENUE AND DIXIE HIGHWAY CORRIDORS OF BOCA RATON FROM NW 20 STREET TO PALMETTO PARK ROAD. WATER ENTERED THE PUBLIC LIBRARY AS WELL AS CITY HALL AND A FEW BUSINESSES. NW 8 STREET JUST EAST OF NW 2 AVENUE HAD WATER AT LEAST 1 FOOT DEEP COVERING THE ROADWAY, AND SIDE STREETS IN THE AREA ARE IMPASSABLE. RAINFALL AMOUNTS IN THE IMMEDIATE AREA WERE AS HIGH AS 8 INCHES IN JUST OVER 3 HOURS.

SOCIAL MEDIA IMAGES SHOW WATER COVERING RESIDENTIAL STREETS, SPREADING THROUGH LAWNS, AND NEARING SOME HOMES IN THE LAKE PATRICIA AREA OF MIAMI LAKES. ESTIMATED 6+ INCHES IN THE ROADWAYS AND ACROSS SIDEWALKS.
WIND GUST OF 48MPH MEASURED AT THE LAKE OKE NORTH END OBSERVATION SITE (L001) ON LAKE OKEECHOBEE.

A LARGE TREE FELL ON A MOBILE HOME IN POMPANO BEACH, DISPLACING 5 PEOPLE LIVING IN THE HOME. THIS WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A BAND OF GUSTY SHOWERS AND THUNDERSTORMS, AND WIND REPORTS FROM NEARBY LOCATIONS INDICATED WINDS WERE PROBABLY IN THE 40-50 MPH RANGE.
